purpose: Use Western civilization as the main reasoning curriculum spine while integrating other civilizations as culture-aware contrast, enrichment, and correction.

coverage_lanes.knowledge_standard: Definitions, demonstration, dialectic, prudence, civic argument, and public persuasion.
coverage_lanes.argument_goal: Truth-seeking, proof, civic persuasion, ethical speech, and practical judgment.
coverage_lanes.disagreement_norm: Dialogue, dialectic, public debate, legal argument, and rhetorical contest.
coverage_lanes.authority_structure: Reason, source texts, public speech, law, civic office, and visible inference.
misread_risks: separating proof from moral judgment too sharply

coverage_lanes.knowledge_standard: Textual authority, formal distinction, objection, reply, synthesis, and ordered theology/philosophy.
coverage_lanes.argument_goal: Resolve or clarify disputed questions while preserving objections and distinctions.
coverage_lanes.disagreement_norm: Formal disputation, preserved objections, replies, and careful synthesis.
coverage_lanes.authority_structure: Scripture, received authorities, reason, teacher tradition, and institutional learning.
misread_risks: mistaking scholastic structure for sterile formalism

coverage_lanes.knowledge_standard: Observation, experiment, method, probability, causal inference, and public correction.
coverage_lanes.argument_goal: Improve inquiry, test causes, compare explanations, and state uncertainty honestly.
coverage_lanes.disagreement_norm: Method critique, rival explanation, evidence challenge, and public revision.
coverage_lanes.authority_structure: Observation, mathematics, experiment, method, publication, and criticism.

coverage_lanes.knowledge_standard: Public reason, legal text, institutional consequence, moral judgment, and historical evidence.
coverage_lanes.argument_goal: Reason about liberty, duty, power, faction, reform, justice, and public legitimacy.
coverage_lanes.disagreement_norm: Public debate, legal interpretation, electoral conflict, civic persuasion, and principled dissent.
coverage_lanes.authority_structure: Constitutions, law, public office, civic tradition, moral principle, and institutional performance.

coverage_lanes.knowledge_standard: Epistemic instruments, inference, testimony, perception, debate, and refutation within school contexts.
coverage_lanes.argument_goal: Clarify truth, defeat false views, defend schools, and in some contexts support liberation-oriented inquiry.
coverage_lanes.disagreement_norm: Formal debate, refutation, school-based argument, teacher correction, and disciplined classification.
coverage_lanes.authority_structure: Texts, teachers, schools, perception, inference, testimony, and debate norms.
misread_risks: forcing Greek syllogism categories onto Nyaya inference

coverage_lanes.knowledge_standard: Demonstration, theology, legal reasoning, commentary, linguistic analysis, and reason/revelation boundaries.
coverage_lanes.argument_goal: Clarify truth, defend doctrine, reason legally, interpret texts, and integrate or contest philosophical inheritance.
coverage_lanes.disagreement_norm: Commentary, disputation, legal schools, theological debate, and teacher-guided argument.
coverage_lanes.authority_structure: Scripture, law, commentary chains, teachers, reason, language, and school authority.
misread_risks: flattening theological/legal context into secular philosophy
treating commentary as derivative rather than argumentative

coverage_lanes.knowledge_standard: Text, commentary, precedent, case distinction, communal law, and philosophical reflection.
coverage_lanes.argument_goal: Preserve, interpret, apply, and argue through law and text while recording disagreement.
coverage_lanes.disagreement_norm: Textual dispute, preserved minority views, case comparison, objection and reply.
coverage_lanes.authority_structure: Scripture, Talmudic reasoning, rabbinic authority, commentary, law, and community.

coverage_lanes.knowledge_standard: Role-aware moral judgment, standards, names, social order, practical wisdom, and restraint.
coverage_lanes.argument_goal: Cultivate order, moral conduct, right naming, practical standards, and sometimes strategic advantage.
coverage_lanes.disagreement_norm: Role-sensitive correction, remonstrance, aphorism, debate in some schools, and indirect critique.
coverage_lanes.authority_structure: Classics, sages, roles, ritual, standards, rulers, teachers, and practical consequence.
misread_risks: mistaking restraint for lack of argument
forcing adversarial debate norms onto role-based speech

coverage_lanes.knowledge_standard: Communal memory, proverb, elder counsel, precedent, lived consequence, and public accountability.
coverage_lanes.argument_goal: Maintain community, teach prudence, resolve conflict, preserve memory, and counsel action.
coverage_lanes.disagreement_norm: Council, proverb, elder mediation, restorative speech, and community-facing deliberation.
coverage_lanes.authority_structure: Elders, oral tradition, community memory, public standing, and lived wisdom.
misread_risks: treating oral reasoning as informal or inferior

curriculum_matrix_rules: Every unit has a Western anchor.
Every comparative lane has a cultural caveat.
Every comparative lesson requires source packet and reviewer status.
Every transfer move must name what should not be borrowed superficially.

build_phases.summary: Build Greek, Roman, scholastic, early modern, and Western civic spine first.
Complete culture framework, reviewer standard, packet system, and curriculum matrix before comparative lessons.
Add comparative traditions only after source and cultural review.
Build proof, disputation, speech, evidence, civic judgment, adversarial, and teach-back labs.
Integrate with open-education-suite, voice-studio, video tooling, source preservation, and QA Live.
